hyperedges.label: PRD AI processing chain (ASR → agent → LLM → CRM)

nodes.label: CallRecorderApp (Android call recorder + transcriber)
nodes.rationale: Single-module Kotlin app, deliberately transparent and owner-operated: persistent recording notification, no hidden icon, nothing leaves the device.

nodes.label: Clean-room reimplementation policy
nodes.rationale: Architecture ideas were learned from Cube ACR's decompiled structure but no vendor code was copied; all code and data are original. Never copy vendor code and never patch Cube ACR's billing.

nodes.label: PhoneStateReceiver (cellular call detection)
nodes.rationale: Starts on OFFHOOK rather than RINGING so unanswered calls are never recorded; receiver instances are short-lived so cross-broadcast state lives in @Volatile companion fields.

nodes.label: CallAccessibilityService (VoIP call-screen detection)
nodes.rationale: Requires both a known messenger package and a call-screen-looking window (exact activity match or voip/webrtc/incall class-name hint) so detection survives activity renames; ignores transient overlays so multitasking does not cut the recording.

nodes.label: RecordingService (foreground orchestration)
nodes.rationale: Only component that touches the capture pipeline; foregroundServiceType=microphone for Android 14 granular types; stopSelf() is deferred until the DB write completes because stopping earlier cancels the coroutine scope and loses the recording.

nodes.label: SourceRegistry (declarative call sources)
nodes.rationale: Holds the BUILT_IN list plus package/activity index maps so adding support for a new messenger is one list entry with no code changes anywhere else.

nodes.label: AudioCapturer (AudioRecord capture engine)
nodes.rationale: 44.1 kHz 16-bit mono PCM on a dedicated thread; exposes the winning source name so per-device capture behaviour can be diagnosed empirically.

nodes.label: CaptureProfile (PHONE / VOIP audio-source priority)
nodes.rationale: PHONE orders VOICE_CALL → VOICE_RECOGNITION → VOICE_COMMUNICATION → MIC because VOICE_CALL captures both ends only where the OEM permits; VOIP is MIC-only because the messenger holds the mic exclusively.

nodes.label: CaptureSettings (SharedPreferences capture knobs)
nodes.rationale: A pinned phone audio source is tried first but the rest of the profile remains as fallback, so a bad pin can never abort recording.

nodes.label: BluetoothScoController (SCO routing)
nodes.rationale: SCO does not carry the VOICE_CALL modem stream, so with a BT headset the capturer probes VOICE_COMMUNICATION-family sources instead. Best-effort and device-dependent.

nodes.label: AacEncoder (PCM → AAC/.m4a)
nodes.rationale: Hardened for correctness: never drops PCM (waits for codec input buffers), monotonic timestamps from a cumulative sample counter, muxer track added exactly once, bounded EOS drain, exactly-once release. Single-threaded by contract.

nodes.label: Audio-source auto-probe
nodes.rationale: Tries each source in profile order and skips any that fails to construct, initialize, or reach RECORDSTATE_RECORDING; exists because which source works is empirical per device.

nodes.label: Silence detection & discard-empty policy
nodes.rationale: A cheap sparse scan flips capturedAudio on the first non-silent sample; blocked capture is digital zero, so an all-silent file is deleted and the user notified instead of saving a junk row.

nodes.label: RecordingEntity (Room row)
nodes.rationale: Stores the winning audio source per recording and already carries a transcript slot so the transcription backend can be wired later without a schema migration.

nodes.label: CallLogReader (call-log enrichment)
nodes.rationale: The service waits ~1.2 s after stop for the system call-log row to appear before enriching name, number and direction.

nodes.label: App-external recordings storage + FileProvider
nodes.rationale: Audio lives under getExternalFilesDir()/recordings/ so no broad storage permission is needed; files reach other apps only through the manifest-declared FileProvider.

nodes.label: Transcriber interface (pluggable transcription)
nodes.rationale: One-method suspend interface with a global Transcriber.active slot; the on-device (Whisper/Vosk) vs cloud STT decision is deliberately deferred because the rest of the pipeline only ever talks to this interface.

nodes.label: Transparency guardrail (non-negotiable)
nodes.rationale: Ongoing recording notification, no covert operation, no exfiltration. Call recording is consent-regulated and the user is responsible for legality; a consent-announcement tone hook is stubbed in RecordingService.

nodes.label: OS mic-exclusivity wall for VoIP capture
nodes.rationale: On modern Android the messenger holds the mic exclusively and the OS blocks concurrent capture, yielding digital silence. This is an OS wall for non-root apps — detect, discard, notify, and do not reinvest.
nodes.norm_label: os mic-exclusivity wall for voip capture

nodes.label: Sideload-only distribution constraint
nodes.rationale: Google Play bans call recording via the accessibility API, so the accessibility detection path makes the app sideload-only by design.

nodes.label: Call Recorder clean-room scaffold
nodes.rationale: Explicitly a scaffold, not a tested app: never run on a device, encoder buffering/timestamps need hardening, launcher/theme resources assumed from the Android Studio template.

nodes.label: Scaffold ↔ Cube ACR component mapping table
nodes.norm_label: scaffold ↔ cube acr component mapping table

nodes.label: Two-app split (accessibility helper APK)
nodes.rationale: Cube ACR isolates the accessibility service in a second same-key APK talking over signature-permission broadcasts; folded into one app here for simplicity. Reintroduce a BroadcastReceiver + signature-level permission only if the detector must ship independently.

nodes.label: Remote-party audio blocked since Android 10
nodes.rationale: No stock API captures the downlink; the ACCESSIBILITY_ROUTED strategy (toggle speaker / MODE_IN_COMMUNICATION) is a fragile best-effort workaround, nothing more. This constraint shaped Cube ACR's messy design.

nodes.label: Legal consent regulation (one-party vs all-party)
nodes.rationale: Consent rules vary by jurisdiction, so the app shows a consent notice and an always-on recording notification by design.

nodes.label: Play-safe subset: own-mic voice memo + transcription
nodes.rationale: Recommended starting point if distribution matters, because it avoids both the accessibility ban and the downlink block.
nodes.norm_label: play-safe subset: own-mic voice memo + transcription

nodes.label: Call Recorder feature roadmap
nodes.rationale: Tracks parity with apps like Cube ACR using a done / partial / planned status marking.

nodes.label: VoIP recording (partial — detection built, capture device-dependent)
nodes.norm_label: voip recording (partial — detection built, capture device-dependent)

nodes.label: Search by name / number / note / transcript (planned)
nodes.norm_label: search by name / number / note / transcript (planned)

nodes.label: Transcription roadmap (the Voice_Transcriber goal)
nodes.rationale: Pluggable Transcriber interface exists but no engine is wired; auto-transcribe after each recording and searchable transcripts are still planned, with on-device (Whisper/Vosk) vs cloud STT undecided.

nodes.label: Security & backup roadmap (PIN, at-rest encryption, cloud backup)
nodes.norm_label: security & backup roadmap (pin, at-rest encryption, cloud backup)

nodes.label: Suggested next batch of work
nodes.rationale: Ordered as search/favorite/master-toggle first (quick, high-value library polish), then transcription (the actual project goal), then format/auto-delete settings, then PIN lock and cloud backup.

nodes.label: AI Call Intelligence Platform
nodes.rationale: Must be fully dynamic with no customer-specific logic hardcoded, so one deployment can serve every tenant.

nodes.label: CRM Service (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho, Dynamics, webhooks)
nodes.norm_label: crm service (salesforce, hubspot, zoho, dynamics, webhooks)

nodes.label: Analytics (calls, conversion, sentiment, AI cost, device health)
nodes.norm_label: analytics (calls, conversion, sentiment, ai cost, device health)

nodes.label: AI Instance (Instance ID, secret, limits, model, language)
nodes.norm_label: ai instance (instance id, secret, limits, model, language)

nodes.label: Device management (register, rename, force sync, remote logout, health)
nodes.norm_label: device management (register, rename, force sync, remote logout, health)

nodes.label: Subscription limits (devices, agents, calls, minutes, storage, API)
nodes.norm_label: subscription limits (devices, agents, calls, minutes, storage, api)

nodes.label: AI Agent Builder (dynamic agent configuration)
nodes.rationale: System prompt, extraction fields, classification labels, lead scoring, sentiment, CRM mapping and validation rules are all configuration so behaviour changes without a deploy.

nodes.label: Dynamic schema (admin-defined extraction fields)
nodes.rationale: Admins define custom extraction fields without backend code changes, which is what keeps the platform free of customer-specific hardcoded logic.

nodes.label: AI processing pipeline (Audio → ASR → LLM → CRM → Storage)
nodes.norm_label: ai processing pipeline (audio → asr → llm → crm → storage)

nodes.label: Model Provider Layer (recommendation)
nodes.rationale: Centrally manage AI providers, routing, failover and cost optimization while optionally still supporting customer-managed API keys.

nodes.label: API endpoints (devices, instances, calls, agents, apikeys, crm)
nodes.norm_label: api endpoints (devices, instances, calls, agents, apikeys, crm)
